title: EvalHub EA2 hardcodes unqualified sidecar image: eval-runtime-sidecar:latest
description:
EvalHub server (v0.3.0, RHOAI 3.4.0-ea.2) hardcodes 'eval-runtime-sidecar:latest' as the sidecar container image in k8s/k8s_runtime.go when creating benchmark Job pods. This unqualified name resolves to Docker Hub via CRI-O's unqualified-search-registries, where the image doesn't exist.

The config.yaml has eval_sidecar_image set to the correct registry.redhat.io reference, but the K8s runtime code doesn't use it — it uses a compiled-in default.

Workaround options:
1. Push the image to a registry in CRI-O's search path (registry.access.redhat.com — read-only, docker.io — requires auth)
2. Use ImageTagMirrorSet to redirect eval-runtime-sidecar:latest to internal registry
3. Kyverno mutate policy to rewrite the image in benchmark pods

Blocker for: running any EvalHub provider on EA2 clusters where this image isn't pre-cached.

Cluster: jeder-evalhub (ROSA HCP, RHOAI 3.4.0-ea.2)
EvalHub server version: 0.3.0

title: Extend Harbor to support Kubernetes Jobs as execution backend
description:
Extend Harbor to support Kubernetes Jobs as execution backend. Harbor currently requires Docker for running benchmark tasks.

Upstream context — harbor-framework/harbor already has related issues:
- #1516: Add Argo Workflows as native execution environment (open)
- #1522: Support agent sidecar containers in K8s (open)
- #1049: AWS ECS/Fargate environment provider (open)
- #1515: --remote flag to run agent loop inside Pod (closed)

Options:
1. Contribute K8s Jobs backend upstream to harbor-framework/harbor
2. Build as a custom environment plugin if Harbor supports pluggable backends
3. Use Argo Workflows (#1516) if that lands first

This unblocks: running harbor-bench as an EvalHub provider on OCP clusters, Ambient Code Platform integration.
close_reason: Delivered via k8s_runner.py in the EvalHub adapter. Bypasses Harbor Docker execution — creates K8s Jobs from pre-built task images, polls for completion, extracts reward from pod logs. Oracle mode proven e2e (reward=1.0 in MLflow). Agent mode (Claude Code) implemented and tested (33 unit tests). See harbor-rhoai/docs/execution-flow-comparison.md for architecture comparison. Upstream contribution (Harbor #1516/#1522) remains a separate future effort.

title: PR harbor-rhoai and agent-eval-harness harbor provider changes
description:
Two PRs needed:
1. agent-eval-harness: feat/harbor-provider branch — adds agent_eval/harbor/ module (adapter, results_parser), agent_eval/evalhub/types.py, deploy/harbor/ (entrypoint, Containerfile, provider.yaml, configmap), tests, pyproject.toml harbor dep group
2. harbor-rhoai: smoketest.sh with EvalHub adapter integration

Both verified with oracle against odh-operator-3290 and odh-operator-3475 (reward=1.0).

title: Plugin cache has stale scripts that reference old EvalConfig schema
description: The plugin cache at ~/.claude/plugins/cache/opendatahub-skills/agent-eval-harness/0.1.0/ contains older versions of execute.py and workspace.py that use the pre-refactor EvalConfig API (flat config.arguments, config.runner as string, config.runner_options as dict). The local project has the fixed versions using config.execution.arguments, config.runner.type, config.runner.* fields. Need to republish the plugin so the cache gets updated. Affected files: skills/eval-run/scripts/execute.py, skills/eval-run/scripts/workspace.py.

title: Extend harbor-bench provider to support Ambient Code Platform
description: Integrate harbor-bench with the Ambient Code Platform (~/repos/platform) for running benchmark tasks. Ambient handles Docker-based execution; results are pushed to EvalHub/MLflow post-session. Investigate using EvalHub local runtime mode (provider.yaml already has local: command defined). See harbor-bench provider.yaml runtime.local.
close_reason: Migrated to harbor-rhoai repo as AEH-jbc

title: EvalHub providers list crashes: malformed pass_criteria.threshold on existing provider
description: evalhub providers list fails with pydantic ValidationError: items.5.benchmarks.22.pass_criteria.threshold Field required. One of the existing providers on jeder-evalhub cluster has an empty pass_criteria dict. SDK 0.1.5 and 0.1.7 both affected. Server-side data issue — the provider registration has a benchmark with pass_criteria={} instead of omitting it or including a threshold value.

title: Create EvalHub provider scaffold for new benchmark adapters
description: Extract common pattern from agent-eval and harbor providers into a reusable scaffold for adding new benchmark providers to agent-eval-harness. Should generate: adapter.py, results_parser.py, entrypoint.py, Containerfile, provider.yaml, configmap-template.yaml, and tests. Build after harbor provider is proven — use harbor + agent-eval as the two concrete examples to derive the abstraction.
close_reason: Migrated to harbor-rhoai repo as AEH-m5i
